steps.title: Identify what the caution signals
steps.content: Researchers "cautioned against applying the findings to customers generally" because participants were all from Serbia, a developing economy. That caution means the findings are geographically limited — and the logical implication is that research across countries with different economic levels would be needed to know whether the results are universal.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'additional research is needed with participants from countries of varying levels of economic development...': Directly follows from the caution: if Serbia alone can't support a general claim, broader geographic research is the logical next step. ✓
  • Choice B — 'customers in countries with higher levels of economic development...are likely to regard package design as relatively more important...': Makes a specific prediction about other countries' preferences — a claim the text never supports. ✗
  • Choice C — 'online retailers...are more likely to increase customer loyalty if they make their deliveries in less time...': A business recommendation, not a research implication; the researchers' caution is about generalizability, not retail strategy. ✗
  • Choice D — 'the greater importance assigned to delivery time...may not be observed if the survey were given to a larger group of Serbian participants.': Addresses sample size within Serbia, not the geographic-generalization issue the caution identifies. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
